description:
You are a Senior Integration Engineer building an end-to-end clinical pipeline for a regional hospital network. The system must process ADT admission/discharge events to maintain a live patient census, then use that census to validate incoming lab results before routing critical values to an alerting system.

Channel 1: 'ADT_Census_Manager' — TCP Listener on port 6661 (MLLP). For ADT^A01 (admit): insert into the active_census table (mrn, patient_name, department, attending_physician, status='active', admit_time). For ADT^A03 (discharge): update the matching patient's status to 'discharged' with discharge_time. Return a standard HL7 ACK.

Channel 2: 'Lab_Results_Validator' — TCP Listener on port 6662 (MLLP). JavaScript transformer queries active_census by MRN (PID-3.1). If the patient has status='active': enrich with department and attending_physician, then route to Channel 3 via Channel Writer. If not found or discharged: insert into rejected_results table. Response Transformer returns MSA|AA (with department in MSA-6) for accepted, or MSA|AR for rejected.

Channel 3: 'Critical_Value_Processor' — Receives from Channel 2. Insert ALL results into lab_results table (mrn, test_code, result_value, units, abnormal_flag, physician, department). If any OBX-8 contains 'HH' or 'LL', also insert into critical_alerts table and write a JSON alert file to /tmp/critical_alerts/.

Create all database tables yourself. Sample HL7 messages are at /home/ga/. REST API: https://localhost:8443/api (admin/admin, header: X-Requested-With: OpenAPI). PostgreSQL: jdbc:postgresql://nextgen-postgres:5432/mirthdb (postgres/postgres).
